outbound_official.notes: Official Norwegian Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Utenriksdepartementet) document titled 'Norske borgere er visumfrie til følgende land og områder' (Norwegian citizens are visa-free to the following countries and areas). Page confirmed via multiple search results but content could not be scraped directly (HTTP 403). The MFA notes the list may not always be fully current and recommends travelers confirm with the relevant embassy. Supplementary per-country entry details sourced from regjeringen.no/no/tema/utenrikssaker/reiseinformasjon/velg-land/ pages. Schengen/EEA area entries confirmed as free movement rights. USA requires ESTA (electronic travel authorisation, not a consular visa). UK requires UK ETA from 2024. China introduced 30-day visa-free for Norwegian citizens. Namibia moved to visa-on-arrival with a fee from April 2025.

visa_free.name: Schengen Area (all 27 Schengen member/associate states: Austria, Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland)
EEA member states (freedom of movement: all EU states plus Iceland, Liechtenstein)

visa_on_arrival.name: Namibia
visa_on_arrival.notes: Visa on arrival, fee of NAD 1,600 introduced from April 1, 2025. Previously free. Source: regjeringen.no reiseinfo_namibia.

eta.name: United States
eta.notes: ESTA (Electronic System for Travel Authorization) required before travel. Up to 90 days. Not a consular visa.

eta.name: United Kingdom
eta.notes: UK ETA (Electronic Travel Authorisation) required before travel. Up to 6 months. Introduced 2024. Not a consular visa.

eta.name: Australia
eta.notes: ETA or eVisitor (subclass 601/651) required. Not a consular visa - issued electronically.

eta.name: Canada
eta.notes: eTA (Electronic Travel Authorization) required for air travel. Not a consular visa.

eta.name: Sri Lanka
eta.notes: Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) required online before arrival.

name: Norway

visa_free.nationality: EU/EEA member states (Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Iceland, Liechtenstein)
visa_free.notes: All EU and EEA citizens enjoy freedom of movement. EEA citizens (including EU nationals) do not require a visa to enter Norway. As EEA members they have the right of residence. Duration subject to EU/EEA freedom of movement rules rather than the 90-day tourist limit. Bulgaria and Romania joined the Schengen area on 22 March 2024.
British Overseas Territory. Bermuda (BDTC) passport holders.

Biometric passport required. ORDINARY passport holders remain visa-free. The Facilitation Agreement between Norway and Georgia was partially suspended as of August 15, 2025 — this suspension ONLY affects holders of diplomatic, service, and official passports (who must now apply for a visa). Standard civilian biometric passport holders are NOT affected and retain visa-free access. Source: https://www.udi.no/uk/important-messages/the-facilitation-agreement-between-norway-and-georgia-has-been-partially-suspended/
Schengen associate state (not EU/EEA). Swiss nationals travel visa-free but are subject to the 90-day Schengen limit when visiting as tourists.
Taiwan passport holders with ID card number on the passport.
Post-Brexit, UK nationals including British nationals overseas, overseas territories citizens, overseas citizens, protected persons and subjects are visa-free but subject to 90-day Schengen limit.

Visa-free status confirmed as of 2026. Note: UDI published a January 2026 page regarding Venezuelan nationals unable to depart Norway due to flight cancellations; this does not constitute a suspension of visa-free access.

eta.system: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System)
eta.notes: ETIAS will be required for all visa-exempt non-EU/EEA/Swiss nationals (including UK, US, Canada, Australia and all other current visa-free nationalities except EU/EEA/Swiss) to travel to Norway and the full Schengen area. Norway is one of the 30 Schengen member and associated states participating. As of June 2026, ETIAS is not yet in force; existing visa-free arrangements remain valid until the system launches. European Commission confirmed Q4 2026 launch (most likely October or November 2026), followed by a transitional grace period of at least six months (estimated full enforcement from approximately April 2027) during which travellers will be asked but not refused entry without an ETIAS. NOTE: EES (Entry/Exit System) launched and is fully operational across all Schengen countries as of April 10, 2026 - this is separate from ETIAS. EES requires all non-EU/EEA short-stay visitors to provide biometric data (facial image, fingerprints) at external Schengen borders; it replaces passport stamping. Visa-free status itself is unchanged by EES. Source: https://travel-europe.europa.eu/en/etias

EES became fully operational across all Schengen member and associated states on April 10, 2026. All non-EU/EEA nationals traveling for short stays (up to 90 days/180 days) must register biometric data (facial image, fingerprints, passport information, entry/exit timestamps) at external Schengen borders. EES replaces passport stamping. This does NOT change visa-free status - visa-exempt nationalities still enter without a visa, but are now biometrically registered. EU/EEA citizens are not subject to EES.

visa_policy.notes: Norway is a Schengen member state. All EU/EEA nationals enjoy freedom of movement rights (no visa required, no 90-day limit as tourists). Non-EU/EEA visa-free visitors may stay up to 90 days within any 180-day period in the Schengen area. No national e-visa system found on official UDI site. Some countries require biometric passports. Georgia exemption partially suspended as of August 15, 2025. Switzerland is a Schengen associate (not EU/EEA) and travels visa-free subject to the 90-day limit. EES (Entry/Exit System) launched April 10, 2026 - all non-EU/EEA short-stay visitors are now biometrically registered at Schengen borders (does not affect visa-free status). ETIAS scheduled for Q4 2026 launch - once active, all non-EU/EEA/Swiss visa-exempt nationals will need ETIAS pre-travel authorisation. Skilled worker permits now require employer confirmation codes (Feb 2026 update).

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit in a Schengen country

conditional_access.conditions: Persons with a valid residence permit in a Schengen country may enter Norway visa-free for up to 90 days. Must bring their residence card and passport.

credential.subtype: residence card in Ireland or Cyprus issued on basis of EU/EEA family member

conditional_access.conditions: Persons who have a residence card in a Schengen country, Ireland or Cyprus because they have a family member who is a citizen of another EU/EEA country may enter Norway visa-free for up to 90 days. Must bring their residence card and passport.

School pupils who are third-country nationals on an organized school trip within the EU may enter Norway visa-free if they are included on a group travel document issued by an EU member state and accompanied by a teacher. Based on Schengen acquis.
Seafarers with authorized identity documents recognized under international maritime conventions may be exempt from visa requirements when their vessel calls at a Norwegian port.
UN civil servants holding a valid UN Laissez-Passer are exempt from visa requirements for Norway.
Refugees holding a travel document issued by Malta, Ireland, Liechtenstein, or Switzerland are exempt from visa requirements for Norway.
Albanian diplomatic passports - applies to accredited ambassadors to Norway and their spouses and children only.
Armenian diplomatic passports - applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
Azerbaijani diplomatic passport - applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
Bolivian diplomatic and service passports.
Chinese diplomatic passport - applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
Indian diplomatic passports - applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
Indonesian diplomatic and service passports - applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
Jordanian diplomatic passports - applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
Kazakhstani diplomatic passports - applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
Laotian diplomatic passports - applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
North Macedonian diplomatic and service passports.
Moldovan diplomatic passports.
Moroccan diplomatic, service and special passports.
Pakistani diplomatic passports and official passports.
Philippine diplomatic, service and special passports.
South African diplomatic and official passports.
Thai diplomatic and official passports.
Tunisian diplomatic passports.
Turkish diplomatic, service and special passports.
Ukrainian diplomatic passports.
United Arab Emirates diplomatic and special passports.

cbi.notes: Norway does not offer a Citizenship-by-Investment (CBI) program. Norwegian citizenship is obtained through naturalization based on genuine residence. Dual citizenship is explicitly permitted (confirmed on UDI citizenship page: 'you do not need to notify UDI that you want to keep your other citizenship'). A December 2025 update references 'sufficient income for reduced residence time when applying for citizenship' but specific thresholds are not published on the main page. Standard naturalization path requires years of qualifying residence; exact duration varies by applicant profile. No passive investment route to citizenship exists. Source: https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/citizenship/

programs.name: Residence Permit for Self-Employed / Sole Proprietor (Non-EU/EEA)
programs.notes: Non-EU/EEA nationals with completed higher education or vocational training may apply for a residence permit as a self-employed sole proprietor in Norway. The business must be a sole proprietorship (not a limited company), must generate profit of at least NOK 341,373 per year pre-tax (confirmed on official page as of 2026). Applicant must actively participate in the business. Permanent residence is available after 3 years of eligible residence. This is not a passive investment program - active business participation is required. Citizenship timeline not stated on RBI page; citizenship general requirements vary by profile.

programs.name: Skilled Worker Residence Permit
programs.eligibility: Non-EU/EEA nationals with completed vocational training (3+ years upper secondary) or university/college degree, or special qualifications through 6+ years professional experience. Must have a concrete full-time (or at least 80%) job offer from a Norwegian employer. Pay and conditions must meet Norwegian standards.
programs.processing_time: No official fast-track scheme. Processing may be extended for vocational qualifications from Bangladesh, China, India, Iran, Kosovo, Nepal, Pakistan, Türkiye, and Vietnam. As of February 2026, applicants must obtain an employer confirmation code before submitting applications (fraud-prevention measure).
programs.notes: Permits for vocational-level positions granted up to 1 year; for university-level positions up to 3 years. No annual quotas or caps. Path to permanent residence in 3 years. If job is lost, holder may remain up to 6 months to seek new employment. Minimum income threshold is NOK 341,373 per year pre-tax.

programs.name: Job Seeker Residence Permit
programs.eligibility: Skilled workers who recently graduated from Norwegian education or are researchers. Must have sufficient financial support of at least NOK 28,448 per month. Must have higher education degree or vocational training.
programs.processing_time: Not specified on official page; see UDI waiting times page.
programs.notes: Duration: maximum 1 year. Period does NOT count toward permanent residence. Cannot be self-employed. Can work full or part-time in any job while seeking skilled employment. If job offer received during processing, application can be converted to skilled worker status at no extra cost.

agreements: EEA (European Economic Area) freedom of movement

CHANGED: Taiwan notes updated - clarified that Taiwan passport holders must have their ID card number on the passport (UDI condition confirmed on official page).

CHANGED: United Kingdom notes updated - expanded to include British nationals overseas, overseas territories citizens, overseas citizens, protected persons and subjects as covered by the visa-free arrangement.
CHANGED: Bermuda notes updated - added BDTC passport type clarification.
CHANGED: Venezuela notes updated - added clarification that January 2026 UDI notice was about Venezuelan nationals stranded in Norway due to cancelled flights, NOT a suspension of visa-free access.

CHANGED: Added 4 new conditional_access entries previously missing: school pupils on organized EU trips, seafarers with authorized identity documents, UN Laissez-Passer holders, and refugees with travel documents from Malta/Ireland/Liechtenstein/Switzerland.

CHANGED: rbi.path_to_citizenship_years changed from 8 to null - UDI citizenship page confirms requirements vary by applicant profile; citing 8 years was an oversimplification not directly sourced from UDI page.
CHANGED: fast_track skilled worker notes updated - added February 2026 employer confirmation code requirement.
CHANGED: cbi.notes updated to reflect dual citizenship confirmation directly scraped from UDI page (not general knowledge).
CHANGED: EU/EEA bloc notes updated to note Bulgaria and Romania joined Schengen on 22 March 2024.
CONFIRMED: Georgia partial suspension of Facilitation Agreement (15 August 2025) verified on official UDI page.

VALIDATION 2026-06-27: Confirmed Philippines ordinary passport holders are NOT visa-free for Norway. Only diplomatic/service/special passports are exempt. Ordinary Philippine passport has a narrow seafarer-only exception. Current record is correct (Philippines not in visa_policy.visa_free).

VALIDATION 2026-06-27: Confirmed Switzerland is correctly listed in visa_free - as a Schengen associate state, Swiss nationals do not need a visa for Norway (up to 90 days), verified via official EC home-affairs sources and UDI.
VALIDATION 2026-06-27: Confirmed Bermuda (BDTC passports) remains visa-free - verified on UDI official page.

CHANGED 2026-06-27: Added EES (Entry/Exit System) as a new eta entry - EES became fully operational on April 10, 2026 across all Schengen countries. All non-EU/EEA short-stay visitors are now biometrically registered. Source: https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/news/main-differences-between-ees-and-etias-what-travellers-need-know-2026-04-28_en

CHANGED 2026-06-29: Georgia notes clarified — the partial suspension of the Facilitation Agreement (August 15, 2025) ONLY affects holders of diplomatic, service, and official passports. Ordinary biometric passport holders remain visa-free and are NOT affected. Previous note was misleadingly general and implied all Georgian citizens may face restrictions. Confirmed via https://www.udi.no/uk/important-messages/the-facilitation-agreement-between-norway-and-georgia-has-been-partially-suspended/

Diplomatic/service passport waiver exact treaty dates and treaty names not published on the UDI list page.

Specific processing times for skilled worker and job seeker permits not stated on official pages; UDI refers to a separate processing times page.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C) – Visitor Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-visa-exempt nationals to visit Norway for tourism, visiting family/friends, or other short stays. Norway is a Schengen member state, so this is a standard Schengen C visa.
visa_types.notes: Norway processes Schengen visas through Norwegian embassies and consulates abroad. Fee is the standard Schengen fee (€80 / approx. USD 90). Must apply at Norwegian embassy/consulate or VFS Global service centres. Applicants must have valid travel insurance for minimum €30,000 cover. Must show proof of accommodation, sufficient funds, and intent to leave before visa expiry. 90 days is within any 180-day period. Citizens of visa-exempt countries do not need this visa.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Business Visa (Type C)
visa_types.purpose: Permits business travellers from non-visa-exempt countries to attend meetings, conferences, negotiations, or business visits in Norway for up to 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Same Schengen C visa category as tourist. Applicant must provide an invitation letter from a Norwegian company, proof of business purpose, and sufficient financial means. Does not permit gainful employment in Norway.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Skilled Workers
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-EU/EEA nationals with higher education or vocational training to live and work in Norway with a confirmed job offer from a Norwegian employer.
visa_types.notes: Requires a job offer paying at minimum NOK 341,373/year pre-tax. Vocational-level permits granted up to 1 year; university-level up to 3 years; assignment-based up to 2 years (max 6 years total). Must have employer confirmation code before applying (introduced February 2026). Pay and working conditions must meet Norwegian standards. Eligible for permanent residence after 3 years. Processing times vary; extended delays possible for vocational qualifications from Bangladesh, China, India, Iran, Kosovo, Nepal, Pakistan, Turkey, Vietnam. Does NOT allow self-employment.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Seasonal Workers
visa_types.purpose: Permits non-EU/EEA nationals aged 18+ to work in Norway in seasonal industries (e.g. agriculture, tourism, fishing) for up to 6 months per 12-month period.
visa_types.notes: Must secure full-time employment before applying. Employer must confirm NAV approval that local/EEA workers cannot fill the role (exceptions: agriculture and forestry). Must leave Norway for 6 months after each seasonal period. Time does not count toward permanent residence. Family members cannot join. Minimum age 18. Stays under 3 months may not require a permit.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Self-Employed Persons
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-EU/EEA nationals with completed higher education or vocational training to establish and run a sole proprietorship (enkeltpersonforetak) in Norway.
visa_types.notes: Business must be a sole proprietorship (not a limited company). Must generate profit of at least NOK 341,373/year pre-tax. Active participation in the business is required. Eligible for permanent residence after 3 years. Not a passive investment visa.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Employees in Humanitarian, Non-Profit or Religious Organisations
visa_types.purpose: Grants non-EU/EEA nationals the right to reside in Norway to work for Norwegian humanitarian, non-profit, or religious organisations.
visa_types.notes: Specific documentation from the employing organisation required. Employment terms must meet Norwegian standards. Part of the broader work immigration category on UDI.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Studies
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-EU/EEA nationals to reside in Norway to attend accredited educational institutions including universities, vocational schools, upper secondary schools, folk high schools, or language courses.
visa_types.notes: Must have an acceptance letter from a Norwegian educational institution. Must demonstrate sufficient financial means. Permit duration tied to the duration of the study programme. EU/EEA citizens do not need this permit. Also covers traineeships, research, and supplementary education for skilled workers seeking credential recognition.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Family Immigration
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-EU/EEA nationals to reunite with family members (spouses, cohabitants, children, parents, fiancés) who are legally residing in Norway.
visa_types.notes: Eligible family members include spouses, cohabitants, children, parents of children in Norway, fiancés, foster children, and full siblings. The sponsor (reference person) in Norway must usually meet income requirements (changed January 2025). EU/EEA family members apply under EU/EEA regulations instead.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Victims of Abuse
visa_types.purpose: Grants an independent residence permit to non-EU/EEA nationals who entered Norway on a family immigration basis and have experienced abuse in the relationship.
visa_types.notes: Applicant must have held a family immigration permit. The permit allows continued residence in Norway independent of the abusive relationship. Documented evidence of abuse is required.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Victims of Human Trafficking
visa_types.purpose: Provides a temporary residence permit to victims of human trafficking and their children to allow recovery, cooperation with authorities, and access to support services in Norway.
visa_types.notes: For non-EU/EEA victims of human trafficking. The permit may be extended if the victim cooperates with police investigations or faces risks upon return. Covers the victim and their dependent children.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Medical Treatment
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-EU/EEA nationals requiring medical treatment in Norway lasting longer than 90 days to reside in Norway for the duration of their treatment.
visa_types.notes: Maximum permit duration is one year. Must have a confirmed offer of medical treatment from a Norwegian medical institution. Treatment must be funded personally or through the applicant's home country public funds (Norway's public healthcare is not available). The hospital must confirm treatment will not negatively impact Norwegian healthcare services. An accompanying person may also receive a permit if necessary. Applicant must intend to leave after treatment.

visa_types.name: Job Seeker Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows recently graduated skilled workers from Norwegian institutions or researchers to reside in Norway for up to one year while seeking skilled employment.
visa_types.notes: Must have sufficient financial support of at least NOK 28,448/month. Must hold a higher education degree or vocational training. Duration: maximum 1 year. Time does NOT count toward permanent residence. Can work in any job (including part-time) while seeking skilled employment. If a job offer is received during processing, application can be converted to skilled worker permit at no extra cost.

visa_types.name: EU/EEA Registration / Residence Card
visa_types.purpose: EU/EEA nationals (and their non-EU/EEA family members) exercise their free movement rights in Norway. EU/EEA nationals register with local police; their non-EU/EEA family members apply for a residence card.
visa_types.notes: EU/EEA nationals have a right of residence in Norway under EEA freedom of movement - not a visa in the traditional sense. Registration is required for stays over 3 months. Non-EU/EEA family members of EU/EEA nationals must apply for a residence card. The residence card is required for travel outside Norway. Nordic nationals (Swedish, Danish, Icelandic, Finnish citizens) have an even simpler registration scheme.

visa_types.name: Permanent Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Grants non-EU/EEA nationals the right to live and work in Norway indefinitely, without needing to renew or maintain a time-limited permit.
visa_types.notes: Generally requires 3 years of qualifying residence on a time-limited permit (e.g. skilled worker permit). Additional requirements include language proficiency and financial self-sufficiency. Permanent residents may travel outside Norway but cannot remain abroad for extended periods without risk of losing permanent residence status.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic / Official Passport Visa Exemptions
visa_types.purpose: Holders of diplomatic, service, official, or special passports from select non-visa-exempt countries may enter Norway without a Schengen visa under bilateral agreements.
visa_types.notes: Countries whose diplomatic/service/official/special passport holders are visa-exempt include: Albania (diplomatic – accredited ambassadors only), Armenia (diplomatic), Azerbaijan (diplomatic), Bolivia (diplomatic + service), China (diplomatic), India (diplomatic), Indonesia (diplomatic + service), Jordan (diplomatic), Kazakhstan (diplomatic), Laos (diplomatic), Moldova (diplomatic), Morocco (diplomatic + service + special), North Macedonia (diplomatic + service), Pakistan (diplomatic + official), Philippines (diplomatic + service + special), South Africa (diplomatic + official), Thailand (diplomatic + official), Tunisia (diplomatic), Turkey (diplomatic + service + special), Ukraine (diplomatic), UAE (diplomatic + special). Conditions vary by country. Diplomatic passport holder must usually not be working at an embassy in Norway.
